description: AI-Powered Error Message Explainer for Developers — Chrome Extension
author: Sola Samuel / Spearhead Finance Ltd

stories.title: Initialise TypeScript + React project with Chrome Extension manifest v3
stories.description: Set up package.json, tsconfig, webpack/vite config for Chrome extension bundling. Create manifest.json with required permissions (debugger, activeTab, storage).
acceptance_criteria: Project builds successfully with npm run build
manifest.json is valid MV3 with correct permissions
Extension loads in chrome://extensions without errors

stories.title: Create folder structure and boilerplate files
stories.description: Set up src/ directory with background/, popup/, content/, utils/, types/ folders. Create entry points for background service worker, popup React app, and content script.
acceptance_criteria: All entry points exist and compile
Popup renders a placeholder React component
Background service worker registers without errors

stories.title: Configure development tooling
stories.description: Set up ESLint, Prettier, and hot-reload for Chrome extension development. Add npm scripts for dev, build, and lint.
acceptance_criteria: npm run dev starts a watch build
Linting and formatting rules are enforced
README documents setup steps

epics.title: Error Capture via Chrome DevTools Protocol

stories.title: Attach chrome.debugger to active tab
stories.description: Background service worker attaches the chrome.debugger API to the currently active tab when the user activates the extension. Handle attach/detach lifecycle and permissions.
acceptance_criteria: Debugger attaches to the active tab on extension activation
Debugger detaches cleanly when extension is deactivated or tab closes
Graceful error handling if debugger is already attached

stories.title: Listen for console errors via Runtime.exceptionThrown
stories.description: Use chrome.debugger.sendCommand to enable Runtime domain and listen for Runtime.exceptionThrown and Log.entryAdded events. Capture error message, stack trace, source URL, and line number.
acceptance_criteria: Console errors are captured in real-time
Stack trace, source URL, and line/column are extracted
Both uncaught exceptions and console.error() calls are captured

stories.title: Error deduplication and storage
stories.description: Store captured errors in chrome.storage.local with deduplication by message hash. Maintain a rolling buffer of the most recent 50 errors per tab.
acceptance_criteria: Duplicate errors are grouped with a count
Storage does not grow unbounded
Errors persist across popup open/close cycles

stories.title: Claude API client module
stories.description: Create a reusable API client that sends error messages to the Claude API (Anthropic SDK) and returns structured explanations. Support API key configuration via extension options page.
acceptance_criteria: API client sends well-formed requests to Claude API
API key is stored securely in chrome.storage.local
Network errors and rate limits are handled gracefully

stories.title: Prompt engineering for error explanations
stories.description: Design a system prompt that instructs Claude to explain errors in plain language with: a summary, likely cause, suggested fix, and relevant documentation links. Include error context (stack trace, source URL) in the user message.
acceptance_criteria: Explanations are structured with summary, cause, fix, and links
Responses are concise and developer-friendly
Context (stack trace, URL) improves explanation quality

stories.title: Response caching
stories.description: Cache Claude API responses keyed by error message hash to avoid redundant API calls and reduce latency for repeated errors.
acceptance_criteria: Identical errors return cached responses instantly
Cache has a configurable TTL (default 1 hour)
Cache can be cleared manually by the user

stories.title: Popup layout and error list view
stories.description: Build the React popup UI showing a scrollable list of captured errors with severity icons (error/warning), truncated message preview, and timestamp.
acceptance_criteria: Popup displays captured errors in reverse-chronological order
Each error shows severity, message preview, and time
Empty state shows a helpful message when no errors exist

stories.title: Error detail and AI explanation panel
stories.description: Clicking an error expands an inline detail panel showing the full error message, stack trace, and the AI-generated explanation. Show a loading spinner while the Claude API responds.
acceptance_criteria: Detail panel shows full error, stack trace, and explanation
Loading state is visible during API call
Error state is shown if the API call fails

stories.title: Copy and share actions
stories.description: Add buttons to copy the error message, stack trace, or AI explanation to the clipboard. Optionally format as Markdown for pasting into issues.
acceptance_criteria: Copy-to-clipboard works for each section
Visual feedback (toast/checkmark) confirms copy
Markdown format option is available

stories.title: First-run onboarding flow for API key entry
stories.description: On first install (no API key stored), the popup shows a welcome/onboarding screen prompting the user to enter their own Claude API key. Include a 'Get a key' link to the Anthropic Console and a 'Save & Validate' button that tests the key before storing it.
acceptance_criteria: First open of popup shows onboarding when no key exists
User can paste their Claude API key into a masked input field
'Get a key' link opens https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys in a new tab
'Save & Validate' sends a test request and shows success or inline error
After successful validation, onboarding dismisses and popup shows error list

stories.title: Options page for API key management
stories.description: Create a Chrome extension options page where users can view (masked), update, re-validate, and remove their Claude API key. Show a key status indicator (valid/invalid/missing). Accessible from extension context menu and popup settings gear.
acceptance_criteria: Options page is accessible from extension context menu and popup gear icon
API key input is masked by default with a toggle to reveal
API key is validated with a test request on save
Key is stored in chrome.storage.local (not synced)
User can remove their key with a confirmation dialog
Key status indicator shows green (valid), red (invalid), or grey (missing)

stories.title: Graceful degradation when API key is missing or invalid
stories.description: When no valid API key is configured, error capture continues to work but the explain feature is disabled. Show a persistent banner in the popup with a link to the options page. Explain buttons show a tooltip indicating the key is required.
acceptance_criteria: Error capture works normally without a key
Explain button is disabled with tooltip when key is missing
Popup shows a banner: 'API key required' with link to options
No API calls are attempted without a valid key

stories.title: Toggle auto-explain and filter settings
stories.description: Allow users to toggle automatic explanation of errors vs. manual click-to-explain. Add filters for error severity levels (error, warning, info).
acceptance_criteria: Auto-explain toggle works and persists across sessions
Severity filter controls which errors appear in the list
Settings are stored in chrome.storage.local

stories.title: Extension icon, branding, and badge
stories.description: Design extension icon (16/48/128px). Show a badge count on the extension icon indicating the number of unread errors on the current tab.
acceptance_criteria: Icon displays correctly at all sizes
Badge count updates in real-time as errors are captured
Badge clears when errors are viewed

stories.title: End-to-end testing on real websites
stories.description: Test error capture and explanation on real-world sites with JavaScript errors. Verify extension works on pages with CSP restrictions and various error types.
acceptance_criteria: Extension captures errors on at least 5 real-world sites
No console errors from the extension itself
CSP-restricted sites do not block core functionality

stories.title: Build production bundle and prepare for Chrome Web Store
stories.description: Create optimised production build, write store listing description, and package as .zip for Chrome Web Store submission.
acceptance_criteria: Production build is under 5MB
All debug/dev code is stripped
Store listing assets (description, screenshots) are ready
